hypothesis: Within the MADE benchmark on held-out ternary and quaternary chemical systems initialized with Materials Project structures, an agent that uses an ensemble MLIP to produce calibrated epistemic uncertainty and selects candidates by maximizing the posterior probability of being on-or-below the convex hull (given MLIP uncertainty) will, for a fixed DFT-query budget, identify at least 50% more novel DFT-verified thermodynamically stable compounds than an agent using MatterGen proposals with a diversity-based acquisition strategy.
